procedures.concept: Candidate micro-skill detected for Linear Equations.
given_required: a linear equation with one variable
procedure: Locate the variable term and the constants in the equation.
Use inverse operations to move constants away from the variable term.
If needed, divide or multiply to isolate the variable.
Apply each operation to both sides of the equation.
Substitute the result back into the original equation to check it.

procedure_steps.title: Step 1
procedure_steps.instruction: Locate the variable term and the constants in the equation.

procedure_steps.title: Step 2
procedure_steps.instruction: Use inverse operations to move constants away from the variable term.

procedure_steps.title: Step 3
procedure_steps.instruction: If needed, divide or multiply to isolate the variable.

procedure_steps.title: Step 4
procedure_steps.instruction: Apply each operation to both sides of the equation.

procedure_steps.title: Step 5
procedure_steps.instruction: Substitute the result back into the original equation to check it.

worked_example.prompt: Human reviewer must approve or author a worked example.

common_errors.label: Sign or placement error
common_errors.description: Student changes only one side or applies the inverse operation with the wrong sign.

common_errors.label: Unclassified procedural gap
common_errors.description: Student response requires human review because the error does not fit a canonical signal.

failure_signal_mappings.review_note: Use only for sign, side, or inverse-operation placement mistakes.
Use when the scaffold cannot confidently map the error.

diagnostic_signal_annotations: Rich diagnostic labels are review annotations only and are not live routing labels.
Failure signal mappings use only canonical live values.
generation_invariants: The problem must target the same micro-skill.
The solution must be reachable by the listed procedure steps.
Generated variants must preserve the same answer type.
Generated variants must remain appropriate for the detected course level.
author_notes: Procedure scaffold generated from synthetic demo material.
Human review is required before any canonical or active use.
review_notes: Confirm concept, formula, givens, steps, worked example, and failure mappings.

procedures.concept: Candidate micro-skill detected for Graphing Lines.
given_required: two ordered pairs or a table with at least two points
procedure: Identify two points or two rows from the representation.
Find the change in y between the two points.
Find the change in x between the same two points.
Divide change in y by change in x and simplify if appropriate.
Check that the direction of movement was not reversed.

procedure_steps.title: Step 1
procedure_steps.instruction: Identify two points or two rows from the representation.

procedure_steps.title: Step 2
procedure_steps.instruction: Find the change in y between the two points.

procedure_steps.title: Step 3
procedure_steps.instruction: Find the change in x between the same two points.

procedure_steps.title: Step 4
procedure_steps.instruction: Divide change in y by change in x and simplify if appropriate.

procedure_steps.title: Step 5
procedure_steps.instruction: Check that the direction of movement was not reversed.

worked_example.prompt: Human reviewer must approve or author a worked example.

common_errors.label: Axis confusion
common_errors.description: Student reverses x- and y-change when computing slope.

common_errors.label: Simple average used
common_errors.description: Student averages values instead of comparing rates of change.

failure_signal_mappings.review_note: Use only when x/y movement is reversed.
Use only when rate is replaced by an average.
